title: Function templates

publication.changeSummary: Language-specific C++: Beginner to Intermediate draft for Function templates; human standards, technical, pedagogy, and accessibility review required. canonicalExplanation.summary: A function template defines a family of functions instantiated for argument types that satisfy all used operations and explicit constraints.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A constrained maximum template accepts two values with a valid ordering and common return behavior rather than assuming every type supports operator<. Your goal is to implement and instantiate a type-safe generic function for multiple value types. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Standard algorithms; Class interface and invariants; Function contracts and return values. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Language rule and operational model
sections.body: A function template defines a family of functions instantiated for argument types that satisfy all used operations and explicit constraints. This rule is applied to concrete C++ source and observable behavior, not treated as a vocabulary definition alone.

sections.heading: Boundary and common failure
sections.body: Deduction does not use every conversion, definitions usually must be visible, overload resolution includes templates and non-templates, and errors can surface at instantiation. Therefore the draft explicitly rejects this beginner failure model: A function template is one runtime function that inspects an argument's dynamic type.

includes: Generalize an operation over types while relying only on documented requirements.
Implement and instantiate a type-safe generic function for multiple value types.
excludes: Deduction does not use every conversion, definitions usually must be visible, overload resolution includes templates and non-templates, and errors can surface at instantiation.

outcomes: Implement and instantiate a type-safe generic function for multiple value types.

examples.title: Function templates: defined case
examples.situation: A constrained maximum template accepts two values with a valid ordering and common return behavior rather than assuming every type supports operator<.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Inspect the concrete source or state: A constrained maximum template accepts two values with a valid ordering and common return behavior rather than assuming every type supports operator<.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.
Step 3 — Verify the conclusion: A function template is instantiated at compile time into type-specific functions after deduction and constraint checking.
examples.result: The defined result follows because A function template is instantiated at compile time into type-specific functions after deduction and constraint checking.

examples.title: Function templates: boundary case
examples.situation: Calling a template with mixed types fails deduction until the interface explicitly supports or names a common type.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Identify the changed source or state: Calling a template with mixed types fails deduction until the interface explicitly supports or names a common type.

Step 2 — Keep this standard boundary visible: Deduction does not use every conversion, definitions usually must be visible, overload resolution includes templates and non-templates, and errors can surface at instantiation.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting rule: A function template is one runtime function that inspects an argument's dynamic type.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Implement and instantiate a type-safe generic function for multiple value types.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A function template is one runtime function that inspects an argument's dynamic type.
misconceptions.correction: A function template is instantiated at compile time into type-specific functions after deduction and constraint checking.
misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Using concrete CPP rules, explain why this belief fails: A function template is one runtime function that inspects an argument's dynamic type.

analogy.example: A system of self-managing components can model Function templates: each component protects an invariant and owns resources through typed interfaces and lifetime.
usefulFor: Connecting types, value semantics, invariants, generic interfaces, ownership, and deterministic cleanup
limitations: C++ overload resolution, templates, value categories, object lifetime, and undefined behavior have no exact physical equivalent.
Deduction does not use every conversion, definitions usually must be visible, overload resolution includes templates and non-templates, and errors can surface at instantiation.
